evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Implement automated testing, data documentation, and observability frameworks
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Rigour signals dominate — automated testing, observability, CI/CD, coding standards, version control — with no velocity/speed language present.
evidence.domain_risk: Understanding of SaaS metrics (ARR, churn, financial reconciliation)
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: SaaS company data role touching financial reconciliation as a desired skill but not a core responsibility, and no regulatory/audit language, so moderate.
evidence.data_team_maturity: the definitive owner of our business logic and the primary architect of our 'Gold' data layer
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Named 'Gold' layer (medallion architecture terminology) with distinct Data Engineering and Analytics sub-teams reflects an established, specialized data organization.
evidence.jd_authorship: Develop and maintain scalable data models using dbt/Coalesce to transform raw data into clean, documented schemas
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Named tools (dbt/Coalesce, Snowflake/BigQuery, Git) with precise architecture context ('Gold' layer) make the day-to-day work very clear.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Support semantic layer and BI data catalogs for self-service exploration
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role centers on the data platform itself — modeling layer, semantic layer, self-service catalogs for internal consumers, placing it in internal_data.
evidence.autonomy_level: the definitive owner of our business logic and the primary architect of our 'Gold' data layer
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Explicit ownership and architect framing over core business logic is a clearly strategic, direction-setting role.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No mention of AI tools for the candidate or AI-consuming data infrastructure anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: Implement automated testing, data documentation, and observability frameworks
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing and observability are framed as an owned responsibility to implement, not just a listed skill.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing: Implement automated testing, data documentation, and observability frameworks
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Moderate: reliability/observability concerns are prominent but framed as engineering craft and cost management rather than compliance or stakeholder-trust risk.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: transforming complex datasets into production-ready tables while applying software engineering practices to data workflows
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Mix of architecting new Gold-layer models (greenfield) and optimizing/maintaining existing SQL queries and warehouse configs (fix_scale) with no dominant direction.
